title: Tapinanthus microphyllus
taxonomy: Tapinanthus microphyllus belongs to the family Loranthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as mistletoes. This classification is based on its unique characteristics and its parasitic relationship with host plants. The genus Tapinanthus is a part of the order Santalales, which comprises a wide range of parasitic and hemiparasitic plants. Tapinanthus microphyllus is a species of mistletoe that is native to tropical Africa, where it can be found growing on various tree species. Its classification helps to understand its ecological role and interactions with its environment.
